questions.scenario: The Orders API has `vpc-orders-prod` with a subnet in `us-central1`. The team adds a worker in `europe-west2`. What is a reasonable GCP network move?

options.text: Create a regional subnet in `europe-west2` in the same VPC network if the design calls for shared network scope.
Assume the `us-central1` subnet automatically places resources in every region.
Create a billing account for each subnet.
Use IAM roles as IP ranges.

questions.explanation: A GCP VPC network is global, but subnets are regional. A new region needs an appropriate regional subnet.

questions.scenario: Which statement best compares AWS and GCP at the basic VPC level?

options.text: An AWS VPC is regional; a GCP VPC network is global with regional subnets.
Both providers make subnets global resources.
A GCP VPC network is the same thing as a folder.
AWS and GCP networking scopes never matter.

questions.explanation: This scope difference is one of the first GCP networking surprises for AWS learners.

questions.scenario: What remains regional even when the VPC network is global?

options.text: The fact that every resource has the same region.

questions.explanation: Global VPC scope does not erase regional placement, latency, or service design.
